results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THIS RESEARCH PROJECT IS TO IDENTIFY COMMUNITY STRESSORS AND RESILIENCE-ENHANCING ASSETS THAT SHAPE RELATIONSHIPS BETWEEN CLIMATE CHANGE-RELATED EXPOSURES [HEAT, OZONE (O3), FINE PARTICULATE MATTER (PM2.5)] AND CHILD HEALTH.ACTIVITIES:THIS PROJECT WILL: 1) WORK WITH NY PEDIATRIC ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH SPECIALTY UNITS (PEHSU) AND COMMUNITY AND GOVERNMENT PARTNERS TO PRIORITIZE STRESSORS AND ASSETS; 2) LEVERAGE COMPLETE PATIENT-LEVEL DATA ON ALL CHILD (AGE 0-17) EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT VISITS AND HOSPITALIZATIONS IN UPSTATE NY OVER 2005-2019; 3) EXAMINE STRESSORS IDENTIFIED BY THE NYS DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL CONSERVATION CLIMATE JUSTICE WORKING GROUP, AND APPLY THE RESILIENCE ACTIVATION FRAMEWORK, WHICH ORGANIZES ASSETS IN FIVE DOMAINS (HUMAN, ECONOMIC, SOCIAL, POLITICAL, ENVIRONMENTAL CAPITAL); 4) GENERATE DAILY FINE-SCALE EXPOSURE ESTIMATES FOR HEAT, O3, AND PM2.5, AND EXAMINE VARIATION IN EXPOSURE-HEALTH RELATIONSHIPS USING CASE-CROSSOVER METHODS; AND 5) APPLY CLIMATE PROJECTIONS TO ESTIMATE IMPACTS UNDER VARYING EMISSIONS SCENARIOS, AND, WITH PARTNERS, INTEGRATE FINDINGS INTO LOCAL AND STATE CLIMATE ADAPTATION AND PEDIATRIC CLINICAL INITIATIVES.SUBRECIPIENT:UNIVERSITY OF PITTSBURG ($120,551) WILL SUPPORT ALL ASPECTS OF DATA MANAGEMENT AND GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION SYSTEM (GIS) ANALYSIS FOR THE PROJECT. ICAHN SCHOOL OF MEDICINE AT MOUNT SINAI ($527,838) WILL PROVIDE LOCAL CLINICAL EXPERTISE IN CHILD HEALTH OUTCOMES, ENVIRONMENTAL EXPOSURES, AND COMMUNITY ASSETS, INPUT ON USE OF NY CHILDREN'S HEALTH DATA, AND ASSIST ANALYSIS OF ASSET INDICATORS AND ADVISE ON THE IMPLEMENTATION OF STATISTICAL ANALYSIS.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS AS WELL AS INDICATORS AND MAPS OF CRITICAL ENVIRONMENTAL STRESSORS TO PROVIDE PUBLIC HEALTH AGENCIES, PEDIATRIC SPECIALISTS, COMMUNITY LEADERS, AND PARENTS WITH EVIDENCE THAT TAILORED INVESTMENTS IN ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE COMMUNITIES TODAY CAN ALLEVIATE CURRENT AND FUTURE CLIMATE IMPACTS ON CHILDREN'S HEALTH. INTENDED BENEFICIARIES INCLUDE PARENTS AND CHILDREN IN UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES IN NEW YORK STATE AND SIMILAR COMMUNITIES ACROSS THE COUNTRY.

ICAHN SCHOOL OF MEDICINE AT MOUNT SINAI ($527,838) WILL PROVIDE LOCAL CLINICAL EXPERTISE IN CHILD HEALTH OUTCOMES, ENVIRONMENTAL EXPOSURES, AND COMMUNITY ASSETS, INPUT ON USE OF NY CHILDREN'S HEALTH DATA, AND ASSIST ANALYSIS OF ASSET INDICATORS AND ADVISE ON THE IMPLEMENTATION OF STATISTICAL ANALYSIS.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS AS WELL AS INDICATORS AND MAPS OF CRITICAL ENVIRONMENTAL STRESSORS TO PROVIDE PUBLIC HEALTH AGENCIES, PEDIATRIC SPECIALISTS, COMMUNITY LEADERS, AND PARENTS WITH EVIDENCE THAT TAILORED INVESTMENTS IN ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE COMMUNITIES TODAY CAN ALLEVIATE CURRENT AND FUTURE CLIMATE IMPACTS ON CHILDREN'S HEALTH. INTENDED BENEFICIARIES INCLUDE PARENTS AND CHILDREN IN UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES IN NEW YORK STATE AND SIMILAR COMMUNITIES ACROSS THE COUNTRY.
